activities_projects.title: Understanding Development Around Us
activities_projects.objective: To understand the meaning of development through real-life examples.
steps: Ask students what development means to them.
List answers on the board.
Students create a small poster showing examples of development (school, hospital, roads).
Students present their posters to the class.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand that development means improvement in quality of life.
activities_projects.evaluation: Check if students included correct examples and explained them clearly.

activities_projects.title: Income Comparison Activity
activities_projects.objective: To compare income levels of different groups.
steps: Give students sample monthly incomes of three families.
Ask them to calculate total yearly income.
Compare which family is better off and why.
Discuss if income alone decides development.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand that income is one important factor of development.
activities_projects.evaluation: Assess correct calculations and participation in discussion.

activities_projects.title: Human Development Indicators Chart
activities_projects.objective: To identify important indicators of development.
steps: Introduce indicators like income, health, education.
Students draw a simple chart showing these indicators.
Write 2-3 examples under each indicator.
Display charts in classroom.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn key indicators of human development.
activities_projects.evaluation: Check whether students correctly identified and explained indicators.

activities_projects.title: Survey on Local Development
activities_projects.objective: To observe development in the local area.
steps: Students survey 5 neighbors about facilities available.
Note responses about water, electricity, schools.
Summarize findings in class.
Discuss improvements needed.

activities_projects.title: Role Play on Development Goals
activities_projects.objective: To understand that development goals differ among people.
steps: Assign roles like farmer, student, businessperson.
Each student explains their development goals.
Class discusses similarities and differences.
Teacher summarizes key points.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand that development goals vary from person to person.
activities_projects.evaluation: Evaluate clarity of role explanation and participation.

activities_projects.title: Bar Graph of Literacy Rates
steps: Provide literacy rate data of 3 states.
Students draw a simple bar graph.
Label axes properly.
Interpret the graph.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn to represent and interpret development data.
activities_projects.evaluation: Check accuracy of graph and interpretation.

activities_projects.title: Sustainable Development Poster
activities_projects.objective: To understand the importance of sustainable development.
steps: Explain sustainable development briefly.
Students draw a poster promoting saving water and trees.
Add a slogan.
Present to class.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the need for balancing development and environment.
activities_projects.evaluation: Assess creativity and relevance of message.

activities_projects.title: Comparing Countries Project
activities_projects.objective: To compare development levels of two countries.
steps: Select two countries from textbook.
Compare income, literacy, life expectancy.
Write a short paragraph comparing them.
Share findings.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand differences in global development.
activities_projects.evaluation: Check accuracy of comparison and written explanation.

activities_projects.title: Picture Analysis Activity
activities_projects.objective: To identify development features from images.
activities_projects.materials: Printed pictures of village and city
steps: Show pictures of developed and less developed areas.
Ask students to list differences.
Discuss facilities visible.
Relate to development indicators.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students identify visible signs of development.
activities_projects.evaluation: Evaluate observation skills and discussion points.

activities_projects.title: My Dream Village Project
activities_projects.objective: To imagine and design a developed village.
steps: Students draw a model of their dream village.
Include school, hospital, clean water, roads.
Label all facilities.
Explain how it improves life.
activities_projects.evaluation: Check inclusion of key facilities and explanation.
